ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general cereal beverage class; no specific entry for wholegrain cereal drink (less sugar, packaged). Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers cereal-based beverages broadly; no specific code for wholegrain less-sugar packaged variant. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged wholegrain cereal drink. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 serving (250 ml / approximately 250 g as prepared beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g) of the prepared wholegrain cereal drink, equivalent to one glass or one carton as typically consumed at breakfast.

nutrient_source.title: Wholegrain Cereal Drink (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for 'Wholegrain cereal drink (less sugar, packaged)', CR ID D04010013. All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this approved primary source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for cereal-based beverages / oat-based drinks
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific packaged wholegrain cereal drink (less sugar). GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wholegrain cereal-based beverages and oat-based drinks, which report GI values in the range 50–60. A midpoint of 55 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 55 × 27.75 / 100 ≈ 15.3, rounded to 15.2 per 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content and classification as sweetened instant drink mix
hypertension.guidance: Despite the 'less sugar' label, this product contains added sugar (3.69 g/100 g) and is classified as a sweetened instant cereal drink mix; individuals with hypertension should prefer the no-sugar wholegrain cereal drink variant and limit intake of any sweetened beverage to support blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage classification and moderate glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: This product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) regardless of the 'less sugar' designation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this drink and opt for the no-sugar wholegrain cereal drink variant, monitoring post-meal blood glucose if any sweetened cereal beverage is consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar, low fibre density, and absence of cardioprotective nutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The added sugar content (3.69 g/100 g) and low dietary fibre (1.14 g/100 g) provide limited cardiovascular benefit; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should choose unsweetened wholegrain cereal drinks or whole-grain foods with higher fibre content to support heart health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients and presence of added sugar
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This sweetened instant cereal drink lacks meaningful anti-inflammatory components (no omega-3, negligible polyphenols, low fibre at 1.14 g/100 g); individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose unsweetened wholegrain cereal drinks or beverages enriched with anti-inflammatory ingredients such as turmeric or ginger.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholegrain Cereal Drink (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-mix or ready-to-drink packaged beverage made from whole grains, commonly consumed as a breakfast drink in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is formulated with reduced sugar compared to standard cereal drinks, providing 58 kcal per 100 g, 11.1 g carbohydrates, 1.06 g protein, 1.08 g fat, 1.14 g dietary fibre, and 3.69 g sugar. The sodium content is low at 24 mg/100 g, and the drink provides modest amounts of calcium (44 mg/100 g) and iron (0.27 mg/100 g). Despite the 'less sugar' label, this product is still classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and clinical nutrition guidelines, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low GI, as per University of Sydney GI Database classification for values 55 or below), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 15 per 250 ml serving. The drink has a mild, slightly sweet, grainy flavour with a smooth texture when prepared as directed. It is typically consumed at breakfast or as a morning snack. Clinically, the product offers limited benefit for metabolic or cardiovascular health due to its added sugar content and low fibre density. Individuals seeking a healthier alternative should opt for the no-sugar wholegrain cereal drink variant. The product is vegetarian, vegan-friendly, and halal-suitable, but contains gluten from the cereal base.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D04010013) with confidence 0.95 as an exact match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cereal-based beverages, documented as such. Multilingual names are independently verified for Singaporean context.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific packaged wholegrain cereal drink (less sugar) are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D04010013 as of 2026-04-16. USDA FoodData Central does not contain an exact match for this Singapore-specific packaged product. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wholegrain cereal-based beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. The product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB guidelines despite the 'less sugar' designation, which is clinically relevant for diabetes and hypertension management.
